macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 7 and the Mishna on Procedure: From Private to Judicial Matter

micro_segments.title: Overview of previous Mishna and introduction to the procedural description
Keitzad oso lah: The procedure begins with bringing the woman to Beis Din
The transformation of a private matter into a judicial proceeding under Chazal's authority

macro_segments.title: Two Talmidei Chachamim Accompany the Couple: Preventing Impropriety and Ensuring Witnesses

micro_segments.title: Why two representatives accompany the couple to Jerusalem: addressing the concern of relations en route
Rav's teaching on three men required during a journey to prevent seclusion
The Gemara's explanation: two witnesses to testify about forbidden relations
Why must they be talmidei chachamim specifically: ensuring proper warning and halacha knowledge
Parallel case of banished murderers escorted by talmidei chachamim to cities of refuge

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Dispute: The Husband is Trusted, No Escorts Needed

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's position: the husband is believed not to transgress
Rabbi Yehuda's kal v'chomer argument comparing niddah and sotah prohibitions
The Rabbis' counter-argument: sotah is less weighty and more tempting than niddah
Gemara's analysis: Rabbi Yehuda cites the Torah directly that the man brings his wife
The central dispute: whether Chazal changed the procedure or merely described it

macro_segments.title: Bringing the Woman to the Supreme Court in Jerusalem: Warning and Degradation

micro_segments.title: Maalin osah le-Beis Din Hagadol: ascending to the Supreme Court in Jerusalem
Me'ayemin aleha: scaring her like witnesses in capital cases are scared
Appeal to admit using mitigating circumstances: wine, frivolity, youth, bad company
The goal of preventing the erasing of God's name in water, not to erase it
If she admits (tameh ani) she loses her ketubah and is divorced
If she maintains innocence (tehorah ani) she proceeds to the eastern gate

macro_segments.title: The Eastern Gate and the Sitara (Nicanor Gate) Procedure: Public Spectacle and Ritual Space

micro_segments.title: Maalin osah leshaar hamizrach: bringing her to the eastern gate opposite Nicanor gate
The Nicanor gate as liminal space: not fully in the azarah, used for purification rituals
The priest grasps her garments: ripping them to expose her, undoing her hair
Rabbi Yehuda's modesty exception: not exposing beautiful bosom or hair
Removal of jewelry and white garments, replacing with black: systematic degradation
Kol harotzeh lir'ot ba v'roeh: making it a public spectacle for all to see
Exception for servants; inclusion of all women to learn as a warning

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Deriving the Role of Beis Din Hagadol from Zaken Mamre and the Limits of Their Authority

micro_segments.title: Mena hani millei: deriving the requirement for Beis Din Hagadol involvement from Torah parallels
Kal v'chomer from zaken mamre: just as zaken mamre requires Sanhedrin, so too sotah
Tosafos's difficulty: the chronological problem of sotah continuing after Sanhedrin left Beis Hamikdash
Tosafos's resolution: the warning by Beis Din Hagadol is not me'akev (blocking), the ritual works without it
The paradox: Chazal foregrounds Beis Din authority yet acknowledges it is ultimately unnecessary

topical_tags.term: Shema yavo aleha baderech (fear of relations en route)
Two talmidei chachamim as witnesses and guardians
Rabbi Yehuda's position: ba'ala ne'eman (husband is trusted)
Appeal to confess: wine, frivolity, youth, bad company
Shelo yimacheh al hamayim (not erasing God's name)
Eastern gate and Nicanor gate: liminal ritual space
Public spectacle: kol harotzeh lir'ot ba v'roeh
Parallels to din: warning witnesses (edei nefashot), public punishment
Zaken mamre parallel: authority of Sanhedrin
Tosafos: Beis Din Hagadol warning is not me'akev (blocking)
Din versus Mikdash ritual: the underlying tension
Yishai Rosen-Zvi: presumption of guilt in the Mishna
Temptation and prohibition: mayim genuvim yimtaku (stolen waters)
Arei Miklat (cities of refuge) as parallel
